Name: Rocket Design Team
Relevance: Missouri S&T’s Rocket Design Team is explicitly focused on rocketry and describes its work as pushing students “to the boundaries of air and space.”

Name: Missouri S&T Satellite Research Team (M-SAT)
Relevance: M-SAT is a student-led aerospace research organization that designs, builds, tests, and operates spacecraft, with work spanning the full satellite lifecycle. A Missouri S&T student-designed satellite also launched in 2024.

Name: Mars Rover Design Team
Relevance: The team builds Mars rovers for the University Rover Challenge and explicitly frames its work around student robotics and space exploration. Missouri S&T news reports it won the 2025 URC.

Name: S&T Astronomical Research Society (STARS)
Relevance: STARS is a student organization focused on astronomy and astrophysics and also runs campus observatory events.

Name: Space Systems Engineering Laboratory (SSEL)
Relevance: SSEL is a dedicated space systems lab at Missouri S&T with work in spacecraft, astronautics, and small satellite development. Its page also ties it directly to M-SAT and nanosatellite/CubeSat efforts.

Name: Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering Aerospace Research
Relevance: The MAE aerospace research area explicitly lists planetary entry/descent/landing, plasma aerospace applications, space propulsion, small satellite technology development, and spacecraft mission design.

Name: Physics Astrophysics Research
Relevance: Missouri S&T Physics lists active work in astrophysics, cosmology, and the LIGO Scientific Collaboration. This is clearly space-related research, though not a student org.

Name: Missouri Space Grant Consortium at Missouri S&T
Relevance: This is part of the NASA Space Grant ecosystem and supports space-related student and research activity, though it is more a support program than a standalone org/team.

Name: Missouri S&T Observatory
Relevance: The observatory is a campus astronomy facility with public viewing events. It is space-related, but it is a facility/program rather than an independent org.

Name: Aerodynamics Research Laboratory
Relevance: This is clearly aerospace-related and includes supersonic wind tunnel work, but the surfaced page is more general aerospace than specifically space-focused.

Name: Space Week partners groupings
Relevance: Missouri S&T’s Space Week page groups STARS, M-SAT, Rocket Design Team, and Mars Rover Design Team together as the main campus space-facing student ecosystem. Useful for confirmation, but not a separate org itself.
